name: pcbtools_example
description: Real 2-layer board (top/bottom copper, both masks, silk, drill) from the pcb-tools examples — a copper-pour-heavy, inch-unit board.
notes: No outline (Edge.Cuts) layer, and heavy copper pours rendered as many zero-/near-zero-width boundary primitives. Adding it surfaced two false-positive classes that are now fixed: (1) min_trace_width / etch_compensation_margin counted degenerate pour-boundary lines as 0.000 mm traces; (2) copper_to_edge_distance measured against the copper bounding box with no real outline. This entry guards those fixes. Several checks (min_annular_ring, min_trace_spacing, mask_to_trace_clearance, component_to_component_spacing, layer_registration_margin) still read ~0 on this board's region-fill geometry — a known, separate false-positive class, deliberately NOT asserted here pending a fix.
